{"action": "submit_and_backtest", "strategy": {"name": "DualMomentumRotation", "strategy_code": "import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from app.strategy.base import BaseStrategy


class DualMomentumRotation(BaseStrategy):
    """
    Classic Dual Momentum Rotation across Equity + Defensive assets.

    Monthly rebalance:
      1. Rank SPY/QQQ/IWM by composite (3+6+12m) momentum score
      2. If best equity has POSITIVE absolute momentum -> hold best equity
      3. Else if TLT/GLD has positive momentum -> hold best defensive
      4. Else -> hold TLT as safe haven

    Vol targeting at 15% annualized on the chosen asset.
    VIX > 30 shrinks position. Inverted curve adds defensive tilt.

    Why vs RegimeMomentumSPY:
      RegimeMomentumSPY reached 0.736 Sharpe but failed at 0.8 threshold.
      Max drawdown worst fold hit 27.9% (above 25% limit). Single-asset SPY
      cannot decouple from equity bear markets. Multi-asset rotation adds
      the missing uncorrelated return stream: when equities fall, TLT/GLD
      often rise, reducing both drawdown and return volatility -> Sharpe up.
      Dual momentum is historically documented at 0.85-1.1 Sharpe.
    """

    def generate_signals(self, df):
        target_vol  = self.params.get('target_vol',  0.15)
        vol_lb      = self.params.get('vol_lookback', 21)
        rebal_days  = self.params.get('rebal_days',  21)
        vix_high    = self.params.get('vix_high',    30.0)
        max_size    = self.params.get('max_size',    1.2)
        min_size    = self.params.get('min_size',    0.3)
        mom_short   = self.params.get('mom_short',   63)    # ~3 months
        mom_mid     = self.params.get('mom_mid',     126)   # ~6 months
        mom_long    = self.params.get('mom_long',    252)   # ~12 months

        n     = len(df)
        dates = df.index

        equity_tickers    = ['SPY', 'QQQ', 'IWM']
        defensive_tickers = ['TLT', 'GLD', 'AGG']
        all_tickers       = equity_tickers + defensive_tickers

        # Discover available price columns
        prices = {}
        for t in all_tickers:
            if t in df.columns:
                prices[t] = df[t].values.astype(float)

        # Fallback: try generic column names, treat as SPY
        if not prices:
            for col in ('close', 'Close', 'Adj Close', 'adj_close', 'price'):
                if col in df.columns:
                    prices['SPY'] = df[col].values.astype(float)
                    break
            if not prices:
                numeric_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns
                if len(numeric_cols) > 0:
                    prices['SPY'] = df[numeric_cols[0]].values.astype(float)

        if not prices:
            return pd.DataFrame({'signal': np.zeros(n), 'size_pct': np.zeros(n), 'asset': [''] * n}, index=dates)

        eq_avail  = [t for t in equity_tickers    if t in prices]
        def_avail = [t for t in defensive_tickers if t in prices]

        warm_up = mom_long + 5

        result_signals = np.zeros(n)
        result_sizes   = np.zeros(n)
        result_assets  = [''] * n

        current_asset = eq_avail[0] if eq_avail else list(prices.keys())[0]
        current_size  = min_size
        last_rebal    = -rebal_days

        def mom_score(ticker, i):
            p = prices[ticker]
            s = 0.0
            for period in [mom_short, mom_mid, mom_long]:
                if i >= period and p[i - period] > 1e-10:
                    s += p[i] / p[i - period] - 1.0
            return s

        for i in range(warm_up, n):
            date = dates[i]

            if i - last_rebal >= rebal_days:
                last_rebal = i

                eq_scores  = {t: mom_score(t, i) for t in eq_avail}
                def_scores = {t: mom_score(t, i) for t in def_avail}

                best_eq    = max(eq_scores,  key=eq_scores.get)  if eq_scores  else None
                best_eq_s  = eq_scores[best_eq]                  if best_eq    else -999
                best_def   = max(def_scores, key=def_scores.get) if def_scores else None

                vix    = self.macro.get('VIXCLS',  date)
                t10y2y = self.macro.get('T10Y2Y',  date)

                high_vix  = vix    is not None and vix    > vix_high
                inverted  = t10y2y is not None and t10y2y < -0.5

                # Core dual-momentum decision
                if best_eq is not None and best_eq_s > 0 and not high_vix:
                    chosen        = best_eq
                    regime_factor = 0.75 if inverted else 1.0
                elif best_def is not None:
                    chosen        = best_def
                    regime_factor = 0.9
                else:
                    chosen        = best_eq or list(prices.keys())[0]
                    regime_factor = 0.5

                # Vol targeting on chosen asset
                p      = prices[chosen]
                window = p[max(0, i - vol_lb): i + 1]
                if len(window) > 2:
                    lr   = np.diff(np.log(np.maximum(window, 1e-10)))
                    rvol = np.std(lr) * np.sqrt(252)
                else:
                    rvol = target_vol

                vix_factor   = 0.60 if high_vix else 1.0
                vol_size     = target_vol / max(rvol, 0.001)
                current_asset = chosen
                current_size  = max(min_size, min(vol_size * regime_factor * vix_factor, max_size))

            result_signals[i] = 1.0
            result_sizes[i]   = current_size
            result_assets[i]  = current_asset

        return pd.DataFrame({
            'signal':   result_signals,
            'size_pct': result_sizes,
            'asset':    result_assets
        }, index=dates)
", "parameters": {"target_vol": 0.15, "vol_lookback": 21, "rebal_days": 21, "vix_high": 30.0, "max_size": 1.2, "min_size": 0.3, "mom_short": 63, "mom_mid": 126, "mom_long": 252}, "risk_constraints": {"max_drawdown_pct": 0.2, "max_position_size_pct": 1.2}, "symbols": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG"], "timeframe": "1D", "description": "Dual Momentum Rotation: monthly rebalance ranks SPY/QQQ/IWM and TLT/GLD/AGG by composite 3+6+12 month momentum. Holds best equity when absolute momentum is positive; rotates to best defensive (TLT/GLD) when equity momentum turns negative. Vol-targeted at 15% on whichever asset is held. VIX>30 shrinks allocation 40%; inverted yield curve adds defensive tilt. Multi-asset diversification targets Sharpe 0.85-1.1 vs single-asset SPY ceiling of ~0.74."}, "start_date": "2005-01-01", "end_date": "2024-12-31", "initial_capital": 1000, "test_type": "walk_forward", "evolution_notes": "RegimeMomentumSPY hit 0.736 mean Sharpe (threshold 0.8) and 27.9% worst-fold drawdown (threshold 25%) — two failures, both attributable to single-asset SPY exposure. Multi-asset dual momentum breaks the SPY ceiling: equity bear markets that crushed prior single-asset folds now trigger rotation to TLT/GLD, which historically deliver positive or uncorrelated returns. This reduces both return variance (Sharpe up) and max drawdown (equity crashes no longer force large losses). Strategy: monthly ranking of SPY/QQQ/IWM vs TLT/GLD/AGG by 3+6+12m composite momentum; hold best equity if absolute momentum positive (Gary Antonacci dual-momentum gate), else best defensive. Vol target 15% on chosen asset. Expected Sharpe 0.85-1.1 based on documented dual-momentum backtests."}
strategy_research:
{
  "strategies": [
    {
      "name": "Dual Momentum (Global Equities Momentum)",
      "description": "Combines absolute momentum (asset vs. T-bills) with relative momentum (SPY vs. EFA). Each month: if both SPY and EFA are below T-bill returns, go to AGG/TLT; else hold whichever equity ETF has stronger 12-month return. Antonacci's GEM variant.",
      "evidence": [
        "Antonacci, G. (2012). 'Risk Premia Harvesting Through Dual Momentum.' Portfolio Management Consultants.",
        "Geczy & Samonov (2015) validated momentum over 215 years of data.",
        "AllocateSmartly.com composite backtest: Sharpe ~1.02, UPI 3.10, outperforms simple equal-weighting across all sub-periods 1974–2022.",
        "Antonacci book 'Dual Momentum Investing' (2014) — 38-year backtest across four sub-periods."
      ],
      "historical_sharpe": "0.95–1.10 (vs. ~0.45 for SPY buy-and-hold over same periods)",
      "market_conditions": "Works best in sustained trends (both up and down). Fails in choppy, mean-reverting markets and brief bear markets under 3 months (momentum signal lags). Parameter sensitivity: 12-month lookback is fragile vs. 11 or 13 months.",
      "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "AGG", "TLT"],
      "timeframe": "monthly",
      "complexity": "low"
    },
    {
      "name": "Meb Faber Tactical Asset Allocation (10-Month SMA)",
      "description": "Invest in each asset class only when its monthly price is above its 10-month simple moving average; otherwise hold cash. Applied equally across 5 asset classes: US stocks, foreign stocks, bonds, REITs, commodities. Rebalance on last trading day of each month.",
      "evidence": [
        "Faber, M. (2007). 'A Quantitative Approach to Tactical Asset Allocation.' Journal of Wealth Management. SSRN #962461 — one of the most downloaded quant papers.",
        "In-sample 1972–2005: CAGR improved, drawdown cut from 46% to <10%, Sharpe increased.",
        "Out-of-sample: buy-hold returned 3.5%, timing returned 4.8%; Sharpe doubled from 0.19 to 0.59; max drawdown 46% → 9.4%.",
        "Parameter stability confirmed: 6–12 month SMAs all produce similar improvement."
      ],
      "historical_sharpe": "0.55–0.75 (roughly doubles buy-and-hold Sharpe in stressed periods)",
      "market_conditions": "Excels during secular bear markets and crash avoidance (2000–2002, 2008). Underperforms in fast V-shaped recoveries (2009, 2020) where whipsaws incur missed upside. Strategy is invested ~70% of the time.",
      "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "TLT", "AGG", "GLD"],
      "timeframe": "monthly",
      "complexity": "low"
    },
    {
      "name": "Naive Risk Parity (Inverse Volatility Weighting)",
      "description": "Weight each asset inversely proportional to its trailing 20-day realized volatility, rebalanced monthly. Higher-volatility assets (equities) get smaller weights; lower-volatility assets (bonds, gold) get larger weights. No leverage required — viable at $1,000.",
      "evidence": [
        "Maillard, Roncalli & Teiletche (2010). 'The Properties of Equally Weighted Risk Contributions Portfolios.' Journal of Portfolio Management.",
        "PanAgora white paper: risk parity portfolios are efficient in classical mean-variance sense; each asset guaranteed non-zero weight.",
        "AQR: risk parity funds declined 18–19% in 2008 vs. 22% for Vanguard Balanced Index.",
        "QuantPedia case study: naive risk parity (SPY/EFA/GLD/IEF) — significantly lower volatility and higher Sharpe than equal-weight."
      ],
      "historical_sharpe": "0.70–0.90 (unleveraged; leveraged versions target 0.9–1.1)",
      "market_conditions": "Works well in diversified macro regimes. Fails when stock-bond correlation turns positive (e.g., Q1 2022 rate shock, Q1 2020 COVID forced selling). Unleveraged version has lower absolute returns than 60/40 but superior risk-adjusted returns.",
      "instruments": ["SPY", "TLT", "GLD", "EFA", "AGG"],
      "timeframe": "monthly",
      "complexity": "medium"
    },
    {
      "name": "SPY/TLT Momentum Rotation",
      "description": "Each month, hold whichever of SPY or TLT had the stronger 3-month total return. Exploit the historically negative correlation (~-0.25) between equities and long-duration Treasuries. Simple binary rotation with a single signal.",
      "evidence": [
        "Logical-Invest Universal Investment Strategy backtests: 3-month rotation delivered 14.8% return over 10 years vs. SPY buy-and-hold, with twice the Sharpe ratio.",
        "Alvarez Quant Trading SPY/TLT rotation study confirms improved risk-adjusted returns.",
        "Negative SPY/TLT correlation of -0.25 is the structural basis — well documented in portfolio theory literature."
      ],
      "historical_sharpe": "0.80–1.00 (approximately 2x SPY buy-and-hold Sharpe)",
      "market_conditions": "Works best when stock-bond negative correlation holds (typical in deflationary recessions). Breaks down in inflationary bear markets (2022) where both SPY and TLT fall simultaneously. Adding GLD as a third option mitigates this.",
      "instruments": ["SPY", "TLT"],
      "timeframe": "monthly",
      "complexity": "low"
    },
    {
      "name": "Volatility-Targeted Equity Allocation",
      "description": "Scale equity (SPY or QQQ) exposure proportionally to hit a fixed target volatility (e.g., 10–15% annualized). Use 20-day realized volatility to compute daily position size. When realized vol exceeds target, reduce position; when below, scale up (capped at 100% for no-leverage version). Park remainder in AGG/TLT.",
      "evidence": [
        "Moreira & Muir (2017). 'Volatility-Managed Portfolios.' Journal of Finance — foundational paper showing Sharpe improvement for US equity.",
        "Harvey, Hoyle et al. (2018). 'The Impact of Volatility Targeting.' Man Group / Duke — reduces tail risk, improves Sharpe for equities and credit.",
        "Research Affiliates (2021). 'Harnessing Volatility Targeting in Multi-Asset Portfolios.' — confirms Sharpe gains in multi-asset context.",
        "Salt Financial (2020): Higher-frequency vol data improves signal; COVID 2020 case: unmanaged -25%, vol-targeted -5%."
      ],
      "historical_sharpe": "0.65–0.85 (improvement vs. unmanaged equity, most pronounced over full cycles including crises)",
      "market_conditions": "Most effective during and after volatility spikes (2008, 2020). Underperforms in low-volatility bull markets (2013–2019) due to staying underexposed. The 'conditional' variant (only act at vol extremes) reduces this cost.",
      "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "AGG", "TLT"],
      "timeframe": "daily",
      "complexity": "medium"
    },
    {
      "name": "Multi-Asset Cross-Sectional Momentum Rotation",
      "description": "Rank all 8 instruments by 12-1 month momentum (12-month return minus most recent month to avoid short-term reversal). Hold top 2–3 assets equally weighted for one month, then rebalance. Universe spans stocks, bonds, gold, and international equity for genuine diversification.",
      "evidence": [
        "Jegadeesh & Titman (1993). 'Returns to Buying Winners and Selling Losers.' Journal of Finance — foundational momentum paper.",
        "MSCI Factor Research (2023): Momentum offered highest Sharpe of all factors over 50-year horizon.",
        "QuantPedia Sector Momentum: 12-month lookback, monthly rotation shows Sharpe ~0.955 with break-even transaction costs of 235–252 bps (far above ETF costs of ~5 bps).",
        "Alpha Architect momentum evidence review: after volatility scaling, Sharpe nearly doubles and momentum crashes are reduced ~50%."
      ],
      "historical_sharpe": "0.85–1.00 (cross-asset universe reduces crash risk vs. equity-only momentum)",
      "market_conditions": "Works in persistent trending environments. Fails during sharp momentum crashes (2009 growth reversal, 2020 COVID snap-back). Adding absolute momentum filter (exclude assets below 12-month return of 0%) significantly reduces crash severity.",
      "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "TLT", "GLD", "EFA", "AGG"],
      "timeframe": "monthly",
      "complexity": "medium"
    },
    {
      "name": "Halloween / Seasonal Rotation (Sell in May)",
      "description": "Hold equity ETFs (SPY/QQQ) from November 1 through April 30 (historically strong 6 months). Rotate to TLT/AGG or GLD from May 1 through October 31 (historically weak 6 months). Optionally combine with trend filter to skip equity leg if SPY is below 10-month SMA.",
      "evidence": [
        "Bouman & Jacobsen (2002). 'The Halloween Indicator: Sell in May and Go Away.' American Economic Review — global study across 37 countries.",
        "Jacobsen & Zhang (2013): Effect strengthened post-1970; present in virtually every country.",
        "ScienceDirect (2024): 8 of 9 sector ETFs show statistically significant positive returns in November/December; confirmed in 1999–2023 data.",
        "Combined seasonal models 2003–2017: Sharpe 1.26 vs. buy-and-hold; annualized return 18.03%, vol 13.26%."
      ],
      "historical_sharpe": "0.75–1.26 depending on implementation and period; standalone Halloween effect ~0.75, combined with trend filter up to 1.26",
      "market_conditions": "Works in typical seasonal patterns. Fails in years where May–October is a strong bull leg (e.g., 2009 recovery, 2020 recovery). Best used as a complement to trend-following rather than a standalone system.",
      "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "TLT", "AGG", "GLD"],
      "timeframe": "monthly",
      "complexity": "low"
    },
    {
      "name": "Composite Dual Momentum with Volatility Targeting",
      "description": "Extend Antonacci's Dual Momentum with a volatility targeting overlay. Run separate momentum modules for equities (SPY vs. EFA), economic stress (TLT vs. GLD), and bonds (AGG). Scale each module's position size inversely to trailing 20-day volatility to target ~10% portfolio vol. Rebalance monthly.",
      "evidence": [
        "Antonacci Composite Dual Momentum: Sharpe 1.02, UPI 3.10 — top-ranked on AllocateSmartly multi-strategy comparison.",
        "Harvey et al. (2018): Volatility targeting further improves Sharpe for risk-asset-heavy portfolios.",
        "Combination of dual momentum + vol targeting addresses both momentum crash risk and drawdown severity.",
        "Antonacci (2014) shows adding economic stress module (TLT/GLD) improves diversification and reduces drawdown vs. equity-only module."
      ],
      "historical_sharpe": "1.00–1.20 (estimated; individual components 0.95–1.10 for dual momentum + vol targeting uplift)",
      "market_conditions": "Most robust strategy across regimes. Still vulnerable to prolonged choppy, trendless markets with rapid reversals. The vol-targeting layer reduces damage from momentum crashes. Performs well in 2000–2002, 2008, and avoids worst of 2022.",
      "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "TLT", "GLD", "AGG"],
      "timeframe": "monthly",
      "complexity": "high"
    }
  ],
  "recommendation": "Start with SPY/TLT Momentum Rotation as a baseline — it is the simplest (one monthly signal, two ETFs), has empirical support showing ~2x Sharpe vs. buy-and-hold, and is fully viable at $1,000. Once comfortable, upgrade to Dual Momentum (GEM) by adding EFA and AGG as the safe-haven fallback; this adds minimal complexity while extending the universe and improving crash protection. Both strategies fit within the volatility_targeting_risk_parity family preference and are implementable with fractional shares. Avoid the full Composite Dual Momentum + Vol Targeting until you have validated the simpler versions — its higher Sharpe (1.0–1.2) comes with more parameters to tune and more failure modes."
}
```

Sources:
- [Composite Dual Momentum — AllocateSmartly](https://allocatesmartly.com/antonaccis-composite-dual-momentum/)
- [Risk Premia Harvesting Through Dual Momentum — Antonacci](https://www.emiratescapitalassetmanagement.com/uploads/2/5/5/4/25541321/risk_premia_harvesting_through_dual_momentum.pdf)
- [Fragility Case Study: Dual Momentum GEM — Flirting with Models](https://blog.thinknewfound.com/2019/01/fragility-case-study-dual-momentum-gem/)
- [A Quantitative Approach to Tactical Asset Allocation — Meb Faber (SSRN)](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=962461)
- [Understanding Risk Parity — AQR](https://www.aqr.com/-/media/AQR/Documents/Insights/White-Papers/Understanding-Risk-Parity.pdf)
- [Risk Parity and Diversification — PanAgora](https://www.panagora.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/08/JOI_Spring_2011_Panagora.pdf)
- [The Impact of Volatility Targeting — Man Group / Harvey et al.](https://www.man.com/insights/the-impact-of-volatility-targeting)
- [Harnessing Volatility Targeting in Multi-Asset Portfolios — Research Affiliates](https://www.researchaffiliates.com/publications/articles/1014-harnessing-volatility-targeting)
- [The SPY-TLT Universal Investment Strategy — Logical Invest](https://logical-invest.com/universal-investment-strategy/)
- [Momentum Factor Effect in Stocks — QuantPedia](https://quantpedia.com/strategies/momentum-factor-effect-in-stocks)
- [Momentum Factor Investing: Evidence and Evolution — Alpha Architect](https://alphaarchitect.com/momentum-factor-investing/)
- [Market Seasonality Effect in World Equity Indexes — QuantPedia](https://quantpedia.com/strategies/market-seasonality-effect-in-world-equity-indexes)
- [Case Study: Risk Parity — QuantPedia](https://quantpedia.com/case-study-risk-parity/)
